action.hypothesis: The routed soft-codebook two-stage family has now improved the frontier at `K=8`, `K=16`, and `K=24`, so the next direct test is whether the same architecture can also take over the `K=32` anchor under the unchanged EF=12 recipe.
action.summary: Run one parameter-only follow-up at `K=32` with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. This cleanly measures whether routing is now the dominant structural improvement across the full currently explored low/mid-K band.
action.self_review: This is the cleanest exploitation step: only `K` changes, and the family has already produced repeated frontier gains at adjacent anchors. The main risk is diminishing returns at higher K, where the non-routed baseline may already have enough budget that explicit routing adds complexity without benefit. Still, this is exactly the uncertainty that the next run should resolve.
notes_to_memory: r94 should extend the routed soft-codebook family sweep to `K=32` after frontier wins at `K=8`, `K=16`, and `K=24`.
Keep all recipe fields fixed and change only `K`; this isolates whether the routed advantage survives at the top end of the currently active EF=12 band.
A `K=32` win would strongly indicate that `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` should replace the plain two-stage soft-codebook backbone across the explored EF=12 frontier.
next_hypotheses: If `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` wins at `K=32`, consider testing the same family at `K=64` to probe whether the advantage persists beyond the current low-K coverage.
If it is close but slightly behind at `K=32`, try one targeted routing-temperature adjustment before abandoning the family at higher K.
If it clearly loses at `K=32`, treat the routed advantage as strongest in the `K=8` to `K=24` regime and refocus search on a different family for higher-K anchors.

This cleanly measures whether routing is now the dominant structural improvement across the full currently explored low/mid-K band.

The main risk is diminishing returns at higher K, where the non-routed baseline may already have enough budget that explicit routing adds complexity without benefit.
